pi_items.source: setup_task.sh generates ogle_alert.txt with this target designation. verifier.py checks for this string in the report.
setup_task.sh specifies RA 17h 54m 32s, which is ~17.908888h. verifier.py uses TARGET_RA = 17.90888.
setup_task.sh specifies Dec -30d 12m 45s, which is exactly -30.2125 deg. verifier.py uses TARGET_DEC = -30.2125.
verifier.py sets COORD_TOL_ARCMIN = 30.0 for angular separation tolerance.
setup_task.sh maps Slot 4 to R-band and alert requests R-band (Slot 4). verifier.py accepts 'R' or 'SLOT4'.
setup_task.sh alert requests 15 seconds per frame. verifier.py checks for exposure time within 1.0s of 15.
setup_task.sh alert requests minimum 15 consecutive frames. verifier.py requires valid_count >= 15.
setup_task.sh alert requests this specific directory. export_result.sh scans this directory for fits files.
setup_task.sh specifies this log path. export_result.sh reads the content from this path.
setup_task.sh creates the initial alert document at this exact path.
Contextual inference: operating KStars/Ekos, microlensing events, photometric pipelines.
Contextual inference: task addresses time-critical astronomical events (caustic crossing).

privileged_info_summary: Verified that this task generates synthetic data simulating an OGLE microlensing alert (OGLE-2026-BLG-0042). The target coordinates (17h 54m 32s, -30d 12m 45s) correctly correspond to RA 17.90888 and Dec -30.2125 degrees. The alert strictly requires the telescope to be unparked, slewed within 30 arcminutes of the target, and to capture a minimum of 15 FITS images using R-band (Slot 4) with 15-second exposures. All output must be saved to '/home/ga/Images/microlensing/OGLE-2026-BLG-0042' and a response log written to '/home/ga/Documents/microlensing_response.txt'.
